full_description:
I need a complete cloud-based environment built on Amazon Web Services. The core of the job is to provision and configure the compute layer—primarily EC2 instances and serverless functions on AWS Lambda—so that my application can scale reliably and remain cost-efficient.

Scope of work
• Design the overall architecture: network layout (VPC, subnets, security groups), IAM roles/policies, and routing that follows AWS best practices.
• Spin up and harden EC2 instances, setting up OS, packages, and automated patching.
• Create and deploy Lambda functions behind an API Gateway where serverless execution makes sense.
• Implement monitoring and logging through CloudWatch so I can track performance and receive alerts.
• Provide concise runbooks or Terraform/CloudFormation scripts that let me reproduce the stack in another region if needed.

Although my immediate requirement is compute, I’m open to your advice on adding S3, RDS, or other AWS services later if they tighten the architecture or reduce costs.

Deliverables

full_description:
We are a growing SaaS and digital marketing agency looking for sales reps who can bring in new clients and grow with us long term.

We offer website design and development, SEO, local SEO, Google Business Profile optimization, digital marketing strategy, Meta Ads, CRM setup, automation, landing pages, lead generation systems, custom web platforms, SaaS solutions, and ongoing digital support.

Our main focus is the United States, Latin America, and Europe, but we are open to any market if the sales rep can bring the client and manage the relationship properly.

This is a commission based role, paid monthly. The structure is negotiable depending on the client, deal size, experience, and sales ability. If a client stays with us on a recurring service, the sales rep can earn monthly commission as long as that client remains active and paying.

We need people who can find leads, contact potential clients, present our services, manage the sales conversation, close the deal, and keep the client relationship organized until our team can deliver the work. Spanish or English is preferred, but other languages are welcome if you can communicate with the client and manage the relationship from the sales side.

Experience selling websites, SEO, marketing, SaaS, CRM, automation, or agency services is a plus, but we are open to anyone who can prove they can sell.

When applying, explain your sales experience, target markets, languages you sell in, how you find and close clients, businesses you can reach, commission structure, and past results.

We want serious, independent sales people who can help us build a real sales team as the agency grows.

I want to finish building a team of voice agents using Livekit and Grok Voice Speech to Speech. I already have the xAI API, a paid Livekit Ship subscription, and Zoho One. I prefer to use as much of the Livekit Builder tools as possible so I can make changes easily later without needing code. Everything should stay inside Livekit/Xai/Zoho as much as possible. I don't want to wire in extra software unless it's absolutely necessary.

Inbound calls will come from my main business phone number (currently on Zoho Voice). The system needs to be fully bilingual English and Spanish. Calls should be recorded, and mentioned at the very beginning of the call. The greeting should play in both languages, then the agent determines the caller's language preference.

From there, the agent figures out if the caller is a customer with a question or issue (battery pack not working, credit card charges, kiosk problems, etc.) or if they're a business wanting to partner. Customer service calls go to Zoho Desk under the Customer Service category. Sales/partner calls go to Zoho CRM (either update an existing contact or create a new lead).

I already have the Zoho Flow trigger and Maya (sales) agent mostly working and firing correctly into CRM/Desk. It just needs some minor cleanup because the Maya agent is currently too open-ended instead of structured data collection. Victoria (customer service) should be more data-collect focused since people usually have specific questions, but she also needs to handle basic explanations (for example, explaining that a $35 charge is actually just a hold, not a final charge). This is important to avoid chargebacks.

At the end of every call, Livekit needs to send the full payload with transcript and summary to Zoho. I already have the agent sending data to Zoho once she confirms it, but I need to make sure that even if the caller hangs up or the call drops before confirmation, the data that was already collected still gets sent. Livekit has this feature built in.

I also want the same agents running as a chatbot on my website (currently Zoho SalesIQ). The chatbot should behave exactly the same as the voice agents so everything stays consistent. I'd like to do this inside Livekit if possible so it's easy to clone and deploy.

Once the inbound system is solid, we'll build an outbound sales agent. It will pull data from Zoho CRM (name, phone, email, company, and background info scraped ahead of time so the agent has context). After the call, it should update the contact record with notes and outcome, then trigger a Zoho flow to send a thank-you email with the call summary.

In total I need 3 agents: Inbound Customer Service (Victoria), Inbound Sales (Maya), and Outbound Sales. Plus the matching chatbot for the website and social channels.

I have two companies that will use this same tech stack with only minor differences in how the agents answer. One is the kiosk company, the other is a financial sales company. The data collected is basically the same, so once it's built for one, cloning it for the second should be easy. Please quote me for doing one company and for doing both.

The basics are already in place and partially tested. This is mostly about polishing, wiring everything together properly, and making sure the data flow is reliable even on dropped calls. A good developer should be able to get this done fairly quickly.

Please include in your reply:
- Your experience with Livekit Agents and Zoho One
- Fixed price quote for one company and for both
- Estimated timeline
- Any questions or suggestions

Ready to get started as soon as I find the right person.

full_description:
My current site, [login to view URL], still runs on PHP 5.6. I need the entire codebase brought up to the newest stable PHP release, moved to a fresh hosting environment, and cleaned of the template-level problems that stop our email and SMS gateways from firing correctly. The upgrade must preserve all existing functionality while eliminating deprecated calls and library conflicts, so every page, form, and database interaction works exactly as it does now—only faster and more secure.

There are a few small enhancements I would like to bolt on once the platform is current, but the immediate focus is:

• refactor the code for PHP 8+ compatibility, 
• configure and test the email/SMS gateway scripts after the refactor, 
• copy the finished build to the new server and point the domain, 
• verify that every transactional message (both email and SMS) sends successfully from the new host.

I will provide cPanel/SSH access for both the old and new servers plus gateway credentials. Please give me a brief outline of your approach, the key steps you’ll take to spot and squash deprecated functions, and the timeframe you need to complete migration, regression testing, and launch.

full_description:
I need a reliable switch-mode power supply that can accept any mains voltage from 100 VAC to 240 VAC and deliver a rock-solid 5 V at 60 A (≈300 W) for a outdoor /indoor LED system application Because the unit will live inside industrial control cabinets, low ripple, high efficiency, and rugged protection features (short-circuit, over-current, over-temperature, and over-voltage) are critical. EMI has to be kept well within the typical limits you would expect for industrial gear, but I’m not chasing any formal UL or CE paperwork right now.

What I’m looking for:

• Complete schematic with part numbers that are actually obtainable 
• Optimised PCB layout and Gerber files ready for fabrication 
• A vetted BOM, costed if possible, plus transformer design data 
• Basic thermal and efficiency estimates so I know it will stay cool in a sealed panel 
• Startup and functional test procedure I can repeat on my bench 

Experience with flyback or forward topologies at this power level, proper grounding practices, and familiarity with controller ICs from TI, Infineon, or similar will help you hit the ground running.

If you have already designed something comparable for industrial automation, show me—seeing a proven track record will speed up the selection process.

full_description:
I’m building a sandbox-style proprietary options desk that runs entirely inside a 15-node LAN. The point is to mirror live NSE-options market behaviour as closely as possible so my team can develop and refine strategies without touching real capital. Everyone on the desk is at an intermediate skill level, so the installation has to feel realistic yet remain safe for experimentation.

Here is what I need from you:

• A dummy trading environment that streams live or near-live NSE options data, routes orders to Zerodha (or an equivalent broker’s API) in “paper” mode, and faithfully returns fills, greeks, and P&L as though we were trading real money. 
• Full support for automated algorithm executions in both forward and reverse modes—e.g., the same strategy should be able to flip direction or hedge automatically on command, with latency and slippage parameters configurable so we can stress-test ideas. 
• Seamless operation across 15 Windows workstations connected through a local switch; no cloud dependencies, everything must resolve locally over the LAN. 
• Clear instructions or scripts for deployment, plus a simple dashboard that lets the team start, stop, and monitor algos, review order logs, and download session reports.
* Net Postions per node / Total equivalent position management etc

PLEASE REIVEW THE .PDF FILE

If you have existing libraries in Python, Node, or Java that already speak to Zerodha’s Kite API, even better—just make sure they compile and run cleanly behind our firewall.

I’ll consider the project complete when we can fire off an options strategy from any node, see it executed in the sandbox with correct market feedback, and flip it into reverse mode on demand without manual intervention.
